pattern: Every land cell has four sides; each shared edge between two adjacent land cells removes two units of perimeter, so total = 4 * land - 2 * adjacent land pairs.
hints: Think about one land cell in isolation: it contributes four unit edges to the perimeter. What happens to those edges when a neighbor is also land?
Each side of a land cell is on the perimeter only if the neighbor in that direction is water or off the grid. Counting those exposed sides directly works.
Either count exposed sides per cell, or count all land cells and subtract 2 for every horizontally or vertically adjacent land pair (each shared border hides two unit edges).

edge_inputs.description: land cell beside water in a 1x2 grid
horizontal strip, shared internal edges reduce perimeter
solid square block has fewer exposed edges than 4 per cell

stress.description: random grids guaranteed to contain at least one land cell
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    cols = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    grid = [[rng.choice([0, 1]) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    if not any(c == 1 for row in grid for c in row):
        grid[rng.randrange(rows)][rng.randrange(cols)] = 1
    return [grid]
stress.note: the per-cell count and the pair-subtraction formula must agree on arbitrary land layouts (multiple islands allowed for stress, both methods stay correct)
